Idiom (Ibanag): Mappia nga lima, napannu ta baggi
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Metaphorical descriptive clause with adjectival predicate
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A hardworking person eventually gains prosperity or success
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Maggafu ti kinaturay ken panagtrabaho ti panagballigi
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects agricultural labor traditions in the Cagayan Valley
First Example Sentence: “Ni Kiko, mappia nga lima, napannu ta baggi, ta awan tu aggina nga inaldaw a nagina.”
Second Example Sentence: “Kunnam tu ama ku, ‘Mappia nga lima, napannu ta baggi,’ nu makita na tu masipag a tao.”
Third Example Sentence: “Maski mahirap, mappia nga lima, napannu ta baggi nu tuluy-tuloy tu pagtarabaho.”

Idiom (Ibanag): Ari ka nga maguray ta bulan
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative imperative clause with metaphorical object
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Do not wait for impossible or unrealistic things
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Dika maguray iti saan a mapasamak
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Moon imagery is common in northern Philippine oral traditions
First Example Sentence: “Ari ka nga maguray ta bulan; umuna ka na nga magubra.”
Second Example Sentence: “Kasta ngarud tu plano na, ngem kinagi mi nga ari ka nga maguray ta bulan.”
Third Example Sentence: “Nu kayat mu nga maragpat dayta, ari ka nga maguray ta bulan.”

Idiom (Ibanag): Nasingan tu mata, aru tu lihim
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: People who appear observant often know many secrets
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ti atento a tao ket adu ti ammo na
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Associated with barrio storytelling and political commentary
First Example Sentence: “Nasingan tu mata, aru tu lihim ni kapitan idi kampanya.”
Second Example Sentence: “Nu mapansin mu amin, nasingan tu mata, aru tu lihim.”
Third Example Sentence: “Kinagi da nga nasingan tu mata, aru tu lihim dagiti lallakay idi.”

Idiom (Ibanag): Mappia tu ngina, madi tu puso
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone speaks kindly but has bad intentions
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Nasayaat ti panagsao ngem saan a nasayaat ti panggep
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Widely used in interpersonal disputes in rural communities
First Example Sentence: “Mappia tu ngina, madi tu puso dayta nga negosyante.”
Second Example Sentence: “Naguyod isuna ngem mappia tu ngina, madi tu puso gayam.”
Third Example Sentence: “Awan ku ti pammati ku ta mappia tu ngina, madi tu puso.”

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Be patient and wait calmly for the proper moment
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Connected to river and tide rhythms in Cagayan life
First Example Sentence: “Maguray ka ta daram antes ka nga mangalap.”
Second Example Sentence: “Kinagi na kaniak nga maguray ka ta daram ta umay laeng tu gunguna.”
Third Example Sentence: “Nu adda problema, maguray ka ta daram ket magisip ka.”

Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Nangato unay ti kinatangken wenno kinatuleng
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Stone metaphors appear across many northern Luzon languages
First Example Sentence: “Nabannug nga kasla batu ni Toling nu maikkan ti bilin.”
Second Example Sentence: “Maski ania ti ibaga mu, nabannug nga kasla batu latta.”
Third Example Sentence: “Kinatawa da isuna ta nabannug nga kasla batu.”

Idiom (Ibanag): Tu aggao nga napia, awan tu linaonna
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A beautiful appearance may hide emptiness or lack of substance
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Saan amin a nasayaat ti itsura ket adda iti kalidad
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used in oral cautionary tales and moral instruction
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Ibanag): /tu agˈgaw ŋa napˈja ʔaˈwan tu linaˈʔonna/
First Example Sentence: “Tu aggao nga napia, awan tu linaonna; kasta tu proyekto da.”
Second Example Sentence: “Nairelato idi eskuela nga tu aggao nga napia, awan tu linaonna.”
Third Example Sentence: “Dika mabaliw ta tu aggao nga napia, awan tu linaonna.”

Idiom (Ibanag): Minanga ka ta uran, naguray ka ta dalan
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Saan a naurnos ti panagplano ket nagresulta iti riribok
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: References rainy-season travel conditions in Cagayan
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Ibanag): /miˈnaŋa ka ta ʔuˈɾan naguˈɾaj ka ta daˈlan/
First Example Sentence: “Minanga ka ta uran, naguray ka ta dalan idi nagbiyahe ka.”
Second Example Sentence: “Kinunana nga minanga ka ta uran, naguray ka ta dalan nu awan plano.”
Third Example Sentence: “Ammu mi nga minanga ka ta uran, naguray ka ta dalan tu nangyari.”

Idiom (Ibanag): Awan tu anni nga awan tu gappu na
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Every event or problem has a cause
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Amin nga banag ket adda rason na
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common proverb among elders in Tuguegarao and nearby towns
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Ibanag): /ʔaˈwan tu ʔanˈni ŋa ʔaˈwan tu gapˈpu na/
First Example Sentence: “Awan tu anni nga awan tu gappu na, kaya inimbistigar da.”
Second Example Sentence: “Kinagi ni ina nga awan tu anni nga awan tu gappu na.”
Third Example Sentence: “Nu adda gulo, awan tu anni nga awan tu gappu na.”

Idiom (Ibanag): Makiring nga kasla manuk nga napukan
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Very noisy or overly talkative person
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Nalaing unay nga agsao wenno maingel
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Chicken imagery reflects everyday rural domestic life
First Example Sentence: “Makiring nga kasla manuk nga napukan ni Berta nu fiesta.”
Second Example Sentence: “Nagbungol kami ta makiring nga kasla manuk nga napukan dagiti ubbing.”
Third Example Sentence: “Kinagi na kaniak nga makiring nga kasla manuk nga napukan ak kunu.”
